nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more collaborative. I'll put Github Copilot on Agent mode, give it plenty of context and detail, watch its initial efforts and understanding, and work with it to iterate.

nodes.label: AI adherence to instructions
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated when working with a GHCP agent in a longer session and it starts disregarding previous instructions.

nodes.label: AI as research accelerator
nodes.grounding_span: AI has accelerated my rate of learning because it's so good at generating samples and documentations and explaining difficult concepts, so I'm not particularly worried.

nodes.label: Personal authenticity in client communication
nodes.grounding_span: I still do most writing of emails and teams messages myself. I feel that AI is capable of doing this, but feels inauthentic.

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.grounding_span: I'm still being cautious, however, to not jump too quickly to trusting AI integration into new procedures.

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI through Github Copilot daily to code up quick but trivial changes or prototype ideas.

nodes.label: Epistemic understanding and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: AI has accelerated my rate of learning because it's so good at generating samples and documentations and explaining difficult concepts

nodes.label: Authentic personal expression
nodes.grounding_span: I personally have a specific level of detail and specific verbiage that I like my messages to have.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with collaborative workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I'm more collaborative. I'll put Github Copilot on Agent mode, give it plenty of context and detail, watch its initial efforts and understanding, and work with it to iterate.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated when working with a GHCP agent in a longer session and it starts disregarding previous instructions.

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: AI has accelerated my rate of learning because it's so good at generating samples and documentations and explaining difficult concepts, so I'm not particularly worried.

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for personal communication
nodes.grounding_span: I still do most writing of emails and teams messages myself. I feel that AI is capable of doing this, but feels inauthentic.

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: I expect it to get involved in more and more complicated processes at work. I expect to generate more complex code with AI and have it become a part of more workflows. I'm still being cautious, however, to not jump too quickly to trusting AI integration into new procedures.

nodes.label: Iterative chunking and feedback loops
nodes.grounding_span: I usually give tasks in small chunks at a time because I find that creates a better result than giving it the entire task at once and creates openings for feedback.

nodes.label: Self-directed learning and experimentation
nodes.grounding_span: I've discovered most of my own processes myself, however.
